WARNING: A2337 (820-02016) M1 Air ONLY. No MagSafe, no Viper/Monaco VR. Two USB-C ports left side only.
data_source: Schematic PNGs cross-referenced with saf-diagnostics-2026-03-02.json (15 logic board cases). Verified against Eason (iphone_cpu_fix) M1 standby circuit reference.

forced_start_bypass.description: Eason diagnostic shortcut: bypass protective isolation to isolate fault location
forced_start_bypass.when_to_use: Board draws 0A or very low current at 20V. Unsure if fault is pre-SVR (charger/CD3217) or post-SVR (PMU/SoC).
steps: 1. Connect PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN to 3.8V externally (4.3V if display attached)
2. Jumper U8110 pins 4 and 5 to force PMU_VDD_HI high
3. Press start button OR connect charger for 3 seconds
forced_start_bypass.if_boots: Fault is FRONT END — CD3217 (UF400/UF500), charger IC (U5200), fuses, SPI ROM, boost circuit
forced_start_bypass.if_no_boot: Fault is BACK END — PMU (U8100/U7700), SoC (U6000), clocks (Y0600/Y8301), ROM, NAND

fault_signatures.symptom: No power at all — no LED, no fan, 0A at DCPS
fault_signatures.likely_cause: CD3217 port controller (UF400/UF500) failure or connector damage
fault_signatures.saf_data: SAF: BM 1170 — 5V column, 'repair logic board one CD chips and charging port'. BM 946 — 'repair headphone jack logic board flex and charging port'. CD3217 is the #1 IC failure on A2337.
check: CD3217 port controllers: UF400 (ATC0 page 55), UF500 (ATC1 page 56)
SPI ROM XF420 (page 55) — corruption prevents PD negotiation
USB-C connector pins for physical damage (page 57)
Burnside Bridge UF000/UF100 for shorts dragging VBUS
F5200 fuse (page 23) — if blown, no input path

fault_signatures.symptom: 5V present but no PPBUS_AON — draws 50-200mA at 5V
fault_signatures.likely_cause: Charger IC (U5200) failure or SYS_DETECT missing
fault_signatures.saf_data: SAF: 3 cases at 5V with charging IC fault keywords. Charging port/IC is the #2 component failure after keyboard.
check: U5200 charger IC (page 23) — check gate drives G1-G7
SYS_DETECT from J5100 battery connector via Q5150 (page 22)
CHGR_AUX_OK signal (page 24)
PPBUS_AON bulk caps for shorts — diode mode
L5230 inductors for open circuit (page 23)
Battery needed — at 5V without battery, PPBUS_AON may not establish on A2337

fault_signatures.symptom: PPBUS_AON present but no PP3V8_AON — high current on PPBUS
fault_signatures.likely_cause: Short on PP3V8_AON VDDMAIN branch or U5700 SVR AON failure
fault_signatures.saf_data: SAF: BM 1111 at 20V/0.5A — low amp case. BM 1067 at 20V/1.4A — liquid damage on logic board. Liquid damage on PP3V8_AON area is common on M1 boards.
check: Diode mode on PP3V8_AON caps (pages 25-27) — compare VDDMAIN vs VODMAN branches
PPVAON_PWR_EN (page 25, via D5340/D5345 delay)
PPVAON_FAULT_L (page 25) — if low, U5700 reports fault
U5910 current sense amplifier
Thermal cam at low voltage on PP3V8_AON distribution
Check power aliases page 79 for full PP3V8_AON distribution map

fault_signatures.symptom: PP3V8_AON present, no PMU outputs — board draws ~0.3-0.5A at 20V
fault_signatures.likely_cause: PMU failure (U7700 SIMETRA or U8100 SERA) or missing enable
fault_signatures.saf_data: SAF: BM 1067 at 20V/1.4A — liquid damage required logic board cleaning. Liquid corrodes PMU BGA pads.
check: SIMETRA (U7700) buck output caps (page 28) — BUCK4/5/6/10/12/13/14
SERA (U8100) buck output caps (page 32) — BUCK0/1/2/3/7/8/9/11
PMU_ACTIVE_READY signal from SERA
PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN input to both PMUs
Power button circuit DB110/QR110 (page 32)
Thermal cam on PMU area for hot component

fault_signatures.symptom: Board boots (draws 1.5-2.5A at 20V) but no display
fault_signatures.likely_cause: Backlight driver, display power sequencer, or display cable fault
check: DP600 (BEN) backlight boost (page 69) — A2337 uses DP600 NOT U6560
UP700/UP705/UP715 display power sequencer (page 68)
Display connector (page 67) for physical damage
Try external display via USB-C to isolate board vs panel
PPBUS_AONSW_LCD — load switch feeding display power

fault_signatures.symptom: Board boots normally — keyboard not working
fault_signatures.saf_data: SAF: 9 of 15 logic board cases = keyboard fault. BM 1149 at 20V/0.8A — keyboard repair. BM 1226 at 20V/2.5A — keyboard + backlight. BM 1451 at 20V/2.0A — keyboard + backlight + housing damage. Keyboard is the DOMINANT fault on A2337. Keyboard backlight part is shared with A2179.
check: BEN controller and keyboard matrix (pages 69-70)
Keyboard connector for damage or corrosion
Keyboard backlight flex — shared A2179/A2337 part
If liquid damage history: check keyboard connector area for corrosion

fault_signatures.symptom: Board boots — no audio from speakers
fault_signatures.likely_cause: Audio IC failure on logic board
fault_signatures.saf_data: SAF: BM 1223 at 5V — 'repair audio logic board' and 'repair logic board sound ic'. BM 1313 at 20V/2.5A — 'repair logic board and audio logic board'. Audio IC is a known A2337 fault requiring board-level soldering.
check: Audio amplifiers (page 74)
Audio level shifters (page 73)
Audio connectors (page 75)
Check for liquid damage around audio IC area

fault_signatures.symptom: USB-C port not working (one port dead)
fault_signatures.likely_cause: CD3217 port controller fault on affected port
fault_signatures.saf_data: SAF: CD3217/charging port repair noted in multiple cases. Port 0 (ATC0, UF400) = left rear, Port 1 (ATC1, UF500) = left front.
check: CC1/CC2 lines on affected port for shorts to ground (page 55/56)
VBUS diode mode on affected port
SBU1/SBU2 lines
USB-C connector physical inspection (page 57)
Burnside Bridge (UF000/UF100) connections

fault_signatures.likely_rail: Multiple — depends on liquid ingress path
fault_signatures.saf_data: SAF: BM 1067 at 20V/1.4A — 'clan logic board liquid damage and repair liquid damage parts'. BM 1366 — liquid damage caused trackpad + charging port + keyboard failure across multiple repair rounds. Liquid damage often hits charging port area and keyboard connector on A2337.
check: Visual inspection under microscope — look for corrosion, discoloration, mineral deposits
Thermal cam at low voltage — hot spots indicate shorted/corroded areas
Check charger IC area (U5200, page 23) — common liquid damage location
Check keyboard connector area — liquid travels down from keyboard
Check trackpad flex area — liquid damage noted in SAF data
Clean board with isopropyl alcohol and ultrasonic before powering
